key_insight: The paper turns hyperelastic continuum mechanics for passive and active tissue into an edge-strain interaction-potential formulation that retains energetic coupling while avoiding full finite-element global solves.
engineering_relevance: This offers a faster, more computationally tractable way to simulate active soft tissues such as myocardium while preserving physically meaningful mechanics.
method: Recast hyperelastic constitutive laws using tetrahedral edge strain instead of independent springs.
Model active contraction via the active-strain multiplicative decomposition, implemented as a time-dependent activated reference configuration.
Enforce incompressibility with a dedicated strategy designed to reduce volumetric locking.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests across multiple hyperelastic laws, verification of energy balance preservation, and application to a realistic ventricle with literature-consistent shortening and wall-thickening.
watch_item: The approach likely needs careful calibration and validation of the edge-based incompressibility and active-strain implementation to avoid accuracy loss in highly complex geometries or near-incompressible regimes.
paper_title: An interaction potential method for passive and active dynamics of hyperelastic materials
